original_hypothesis.title: No Emergency Unstake Path — Paused Contract Permanently Traps All Custodied NFTs
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Both stakeMunchable and unstakeMunchable are gated by the notPaused modifier. There is no emergency withdrawal function, rescue mechanism, or pause-exempt unstake path. If the contract is paused (legitimately or maliciously) and cannot be unpaused (admin key loss, governance deadlock, or intentional griefing by pause authority), all NFTs held in custody are permanently locked with no recovery path.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The contract holds custodied ERC721 NFTs via transferFrom. The only exit path (unstakeMunchable) requires the contract to be unpaused. No emergency rescue function exists in the visible code.

proof_of_signal.pattern: unstakeMunchable has notPaused modifier; no alternative unstake or emergency rescue function exists in contract

preconditions: Pause cannot be lifted (admin unavailability, key compromise, governance deadlock)
Users have staked NFTs in the contract

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: NFT custody locked behind same pause gate as all other operations — no separate emergency path
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Pause authority (inherited from BaseBlastManagerUpgradeable) controls all NFT recovery
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Duration of pause; permanent if unpause is impossible

operational_questions: Who controls the pause/unpause authority in BaseBlastManagerUpgradeable?
Is there a timelock or multisig on pause authority?
Does the proxy allow upgrade during pause to add emergency withdrawal?
validation_requirements: Review BaseBlastManagerUpgradeable for pause/unpause access control
Verify no emergency withdrawal in base contracts
Test: pause contract, attempt unstakeMunchable — verify revert

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Pause authority pauses the contract (legitimately or through compromise)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All unstakeMunchable calls revert with pause check
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: NFT owners cannot recover their assets
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: If pause authority is lost, NFTs are permanently locked
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires pause authority compromise or admin error

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Pause authority is the single point of failure for all NFT recovery
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All custodied NFTs inaccessible during pause
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High if unpause authority is lost; otherwise recoverable on unpause

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
